title: Japanese singer and actor Miwa Akihiro dies at 91
summary: Known for 'Yoitomake no Uta' and his work as a voice actor, he leaves a cultural legacy in Japan.

title: US and Iran intensify mutual attacks in war escalation
summary: Washington bombs Iranian facilities and Tehran responds against US bases in Bahrain and Kuwait, amid fragile ceasefire attempts.

title: Yoshinobu Yamamoto earns his eighth win and Shohei Ohtani gets two hits in Dodgers victory
summary: The Dodgers defeat the Padres 15-3 with a solid performance by the Japanese pitcher and offensive support from Ohtani.

title: Magnitude 6.1 earthquake shakes Aomori and Iwate prefectures
summary: The earthquake of intensity 5 on the Japanese scale did not cause a tsunami, but authorities urge caution for the next week.

title: World Cup anecdotes and curiosities: from socks to Brazilian coffee
summary: The World Cup generates stories like the warning about Nakamura's socks, uniform rankings, and coffee promotions.

title: Part of Hikone Castle's stone wall collapses due to heavy rains
summary: The collapse of about 6.5 meters in the inner moat was likely caused by rains from Typhoons 7 and 8.

title: Earthquake in Venezuela surpasses 1,400 deaths and causes severe economic damage
summary: Consecutive earthquakes devastated the capital and surrounding areas, with more than 400 aftershocks hampering rescue.

title: Asian teams suffer tough eliminations in World Cup group stage
summary: Iran, South Korea, and seven other Asian teams are eliminated from the tournament, reflecting the continent's poor performance.

title: Coaches and players analyze their teams' performance at the World Cup
summary: From Spain's confidence to Cape Verde's determination, teams prepare for the round of 16.

title: Political confrontation increases in Japan as parliamentary session closure approaches
summary: Government and opposition clash over electoral reform and disinformation amidst local elections.

title: Japan strengthens its defense with military exercises and plans for unmanned submarines
summary: The Self-Defense Forces conduct drills with the US and evaluate deploying autonomous submarines in the Pacific.

title: Various sports disciplines capture attention in Japan
summary: From high school baseball to golf and horse racing, the country follows multiple competitions.

title: England and Colombia advance to round of 16; Congo achieves first World Cup victory
summary: In a World Cup full of emotions, teams secure their passage to the next round with convincing performances.

title: Messi sets World Cup goal record and Dembélé scores a hat-trick
summary: Lionel Messi becomes the first player to score in seven consecutive tournament matches, while Ousmane Dembélé adds three goals.

title: Mayor of Nasu contests annulment of his electoral victory by one vote
summary: The mayor elected by a margin of a single vote files a legal appeal after the election commission's decision.

title: Search continues for 5-year-old boy missing at a spa in Japan
summary: The mother expresses deep remorse while rescue teams expand the search area after a week.

title: Fire in Akita destroys home and causes death of elderly couple
summary: The fire completely consumed a house in Katagami and it is feared that the residents, an elderly couple, have died.

title: Heatwave in Europe leaves over a hundred dead and historic temperatures
summary: Germany reaches 41.5 degrees and Paris records 109 deaths amid unprecedented heatwave.

title: Japan promotes autonomous driving to eliminate areas without public transport
summary: The government sets a goal of 2029 to cover transport gaps with autonomous vehicles, while data is collected in Osaka.

title: Japanese economy faces challenges amid wages, currencies, and military spending
summary: From differences in minimum wage to increased currency trading, Japan's economy seeks stability in the face of global conflicts.
